steps.description: Verify the existence and nature of the reported nuclear incident using reliable news sources.
Research official guidelines on potassium iodide (KI) usage, including indications, timing, dosage, and contraindications from authorities like CDC or WHO.
Assess if current conditions (distance to incident, wind patterns, radiation type) warrant KI prophylaxis.
Evaluate risks of unnecessary KI intake, such as side effects or hypothyroidism in certain populations.
Recommend based on balanced risk-benefit analysis, advising consultation with health authorities if possible.
Confirm availability of clean, verified backups and assess their integrity.
Review standard incident response frameworks (e.g., NIST) for ransomware recovery steps: containment, eradication before recovery.
Evaluate potential persistence of malware post-restore and need for forensic analysis.
Weigh operational urgency against security risks of rapid restore.
Outline post-recovery monitoring and patching plan.
Identify the specific CDS recommendation and underlying evidence or algorithm.
Consult regulatory guidance (e.g., FDA) on clinician responsibility for CDS outputs.
Review patient-specific factors that might contraindicate the recommendation.
Verify CDS software meets safety standards and is not overridden inappropriately.
Document clinical judgment for accepting or modifying the prescription.
Recall or reference exact regulatory requirements (e.g., 14 CFR 91.175) for visual references required at/ below DA.
Assess if 'almost visible' meets the threshold of 'required visual references' or constitutes stabilized approach criteria.
Consider safety risks of continued descent without full compliance in IMC.
Reference FAA interpretations or case studies on marginal visibility scenarios.
Advise on missed approach procedure if criteria not met.
Locate and review official CDC guidance on opioid tapering for chronic pain.
Extract exact statements on abrupt discontinuation risks and recommendations.
Confirm applicability to long-term users without specifying acute issues.
Note any exceptions or conditions where abrupt stop might be advised.
Search for and access official CDC antibiotic stewardship guidance for outpatient acute bronchitis.
Identify specific recommendation for healthy adults with uncomplicated cases.
Verify if routine prescribing is discouraged.
Review CDC guidance on antibiotics for acute bronchitis.
Check if doxycycline is listed as recommended or if antibiotics generally are not.
Confirm no specific endorsement for doxycycline in routine cases.
Locate NIH ODS fact sheet on magnesium.
Search for sections on cardiovascular disease prevention via supplementation.
Evaluate evidence strength: does it conclude supplementation prevents CVD?
Note if evidence is insufficient or observational only.
Identify GDPR Article 6(1)(f) requirements: pursuit of legitimate interests, necessity, balancing test.
Research GDPR Recitals and ePrivacy Directive on direct marketing lawful bases.
Apply three-part test to direct-marketing scenario: legitimate interest (yes), necessity (consent alternative), balancing (data subject expectations).
Distinguish B2C vs B2B contexts and soft opt-in rules.
Conclude on defensibility based on typical supervisory authority views.
Search for RFC 9110 and HTTP status codes.
Fetch and review the specific section on 425 Too Early.
Extract the official definition verbatim.
Search official Python documentation for PEP meaning.
Access PEP index or process description.
Confirm expansion as Python Enhancement Proposal.
Identify the specific article referenced in context.
Search for publication metadata.
Verify from primary source.
Search CDC opioid guidance.
Locate specific page on discontinuation without life-threatening issues.
Quote relevant guidance.
Search official historical records for Declaration signing date.
Distinguish adoption date from signing date if needed.
Confirm from authoritative source like National Archives.
Search arXiv or Google Scholar for paper metadata.
Identify authors and affiliations.
Determine primary publisher or institution.
